title: The War of Art
subtitle: Break Through the Blocks and Win Your Inner Creative Battles
excerpt: Steven Pressfield identifies the universal force of Resistance that blocks all creative work, and shows how treating yourself like a professional — showing up every day regardless of fear or inspiration — is the only path through it.
whoShouldRead: Writers, artists, and creators of all kinds facing creative blocks
Anyone who has a great idea but cannot bring themselves to start
People recovering from addiction or self-defeating behaviour
Anyone seeking to understand why fear arises before important work
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking a step-by-step productivity system with tactical exercises
People looking for clinical CBT techniques for anxiety or OCD
Those uninterested in a philosophical or spiritual framing of creative struggle
keyTakeaways: Resistance is universal, impersonal, and always points toward what you most need to do
You do not need inspiration to do the work — showing up is enough
The professional works whether or not they feel like it; the amateur waits for the muse
Resistance manifests as procrastination, self-sabotage, addiction, and rationalisation
The desk is where the battle is fought; establish a routine and show up every day
The professional loves the mean — the process, practice — not just the outcome
Staying in the pocket means refusing to let Resistance push you out of your work

relatedBooks.title: Deep Work
relatedBooks.author: Cal Newport
relatedBooks.reason: Cal Newport's rules for focused success complement Pressfield's insistence on showing up daily without waiting for inspiration.

relatedBooks.title: Atomic Habits
relatedBooks.author: James Clear
relatedBooks.reason: James Clear's framework for small daily actions aligns with the professional's commitment to routine.

relatedBooks.title: Flow
relatedBooks.author: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi
relatedBooks.reason: Flow theory explains the psychological state Pressfield describes when the professional has turned up long enough for inspiration to arrive.

relatedBooks.title: The Artist's Way
relatedBooks.author: Julia Cameron
relatedBooks.reason: Julia Cameron's 12-week creativity recovery system is a practical descendant of the same insight: fear is the enemy and routine is the cure.

relatedBooks.title: On Writing
relatedBooks.author: Stephen King
relatedBooks.reason: Stephen King's memoir on craft echoes Pressfield's insistence on showing up daily and treating writing as work, not magic.
